dataset: Legut et al. 2022 Nature (Sanjana lab), GSE193736 bulk RNA-seq
design: LTBR overexpression vs tNGFR control, primary human CD4+ and CD8+ T cells, rest & stim, n=3 each
independence: fully arm's-length: different lab (Sanjana/NYU), different perturbation modality (ORF overexpression, not CRISPRi knockdown)
test: LTBR is a top proliferation/NF-kB driver; overexpression should RAISE the pro-inflammatory program (mirror of a driver-antagonize knockdown)
caveat: Single-gene (LTBR) directional test, not a genome-scale concordance panel; overexpression modality differs from our knockdowns. It confirms the program+direction machinery on independent human-CD4 data, but is one perturbation, not many.
interpretation: Independent, arm's-length confirmation that the directional program score responds correctly to a known driver in the predicted direction, in the same cell type (human CD4) as the source atlas, from a different lab and opposite perturbation modality.
